title: Ensure that multi-factor authentication is enabled for all RAM users  that have a console password

input_row.title: Ensure that multi-factor authentication is enabled for all RAM users  that have a console password
input_row.description: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user logs on to Alibaba Cloud, they will be prompted for their user name and password followed by an authentication code from their virtual MFA device. It is recommended that MFA be enabled for all users that have a console password.
input_row.rationale: MFA requires users to verify their identities by entering two authentication factors. When MFA is enabled, an attacker faces at least two different authentication mechanisms. The additional security makes it harder for an attacker to gain access to protected resources or data.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if an MFA device is enabled for all RAM users having a console password: Through the management console: 1. Logon to RAM console. 2. Choose Identities > Users. 3. In the User Logon Name/Display Name column, click the username of each RAM user. 4. In the Console Logon Management section, if Console Access is set to Enabled, make sure that Required to Enable MFA is set to Yes. Through the CLI Run the following command to determine if an MFA device is enabled for a RAM user: aliyun ram GetUserMFAInfo --UserName Note: If an error is reported, no MFA device is enabled for the RAM user. 20 | P a g e
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to determine if an MFA device is enabled for all RAM users having a console password: Through the management console: 1. Logon to RAM console. 2. Choose Identities > Users. 3. In the User Logon Name/Display Name column, click the username of each RAM user. 4. In the Console Logon Management section, click Modify Logon Settings. 5. Select Enabled for Console Password Logon, and Required for Enable MFA. Note: After you select Enabled for Console Password Logon, and Required for Enable MFA when modifying the logon settings of a RAM user, the user can go to step 7 when logging on to the RAM console for the first time. 6. In the MFA Device section, click Enable the device. 7. Download and install Google Authenticator on your mobile phone. o For iOS: Install Google Authenticator from the App Store. o For Android: Install Google Authenticator from the Google Play Store. Note: You need to install a QR code scanner from the Google Play Store for Google Authenticator to identify QR codes. 8. Open Google Authenticator and tap BEGIN SETUP. o Tap Scan barcode and scan the QR code displayed on the Scan the code tab in the console. o Tap Manual entry, enter the username and key, and then tap the check mark (√) icon. Note: You can obtain the username and key from the Retrieval manually enter information tab in the console. 9. On the Scan the code tab, enter the two consecutive security codes obtained from Google Authenticator and click Enable. Note: The security code is refreshed at an interval of 30 seconds. For more information, see Enable an MFA device for a RAM user.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- Based on the audit field, it specifies using Aliyun CLI commands, which indicates that the control can be verified through API checks.
- The presence of an exact CLI command in the audit section (`aliyun ram GetUserMFAInfo --UserName`) confirms that it can be automated.
- The control is related to IAM (Resource Access Management), a standard Alibaba Cloud service property, making it subject to automation checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ram_user_mfa_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Aliyun CLI command `aliyun ram GetUserMFAInfo --UserName <UserName>` to check if an MFA device is enabled for each RAM user with a console password.
- The program should enumerate all RAM users by using the command `aliyun ram ListUsers`.
- For each RAM user retrieved, the program will retrieve MFA info using the aforementioned command.
- Pass/Fail criteria will be:
  - **Pass**: If the response indicates that the user has an MFA device enabled, with "MFAStatus" as `Enabled`.
  - **Fail**: If an error is reported or "MFAStatus" is `NotEnabled`.

Steps for implementation:
1. Enumerate all RAM users using `aliyun ram ListUsers`.
2. For each user, execute `aliyun ram GetUserMFAInfo --UserName <UserName>`.
3. Check the output for each user to determine if the MFA is enabled.

This approach ensures a comprehensive automated verification of the MFA status for all RAM users who have console passwords on Alibaba Cloud.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of Aliyun CLI commands to check MFA status for RAM users is a valid method for automation.
- The control can indeed be checked via Alibaba Cloud APIs/Aliyun CLI/CLI. The use of `aliyun ram ListUsers` and `aliyun ram GetUserMFAInfo` commands is appropriate for this task.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically correct. The Aliyun CLI commands specified (`aliyun ram ListUsers` and `aliyun ram GetUserMFAInfo --UserName <UserName>`) are valid and correctly used to verify MFA status.
- The manual steps outlined for the automation process are accurate and complete.
- The program name `ali_ram_user_mfa_enabled` is descriptive and follows the convention, though it could be slightly improved for clarity.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to be more descriptive, such as `alicloud_ram_user_mfa_status_check`, to clearly indicate that it is checking the status of MFA for RAM users.
- Consider adding error handling in the automation script to manage potential API call failures or rate limits.
- It may be beneficial to include logging or reporting functionality to document which users have MFA enabled or not, for audit trail purposes.
- Ensure that the script checks for users with console passwords specifically, as the control is focused on those users.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of Aliyun CLI for automation is appropriate and technically sound.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `alicloud_ram_user_mfa_status_check` for better clarity.
- Keep the automated approach as it is effective and efficient for this control.
